questions.question: Mac Donald Award was announced in the year
questions.explanation: After the Second Round Table Conference, Gandhiji was arrested and in the meantime, British PM Ramsay Mac Donald announced Communal Award in which separate representation of Sikhs, Muslims, Christians etc has been conceptualized and there was also separate representation for Depressed Classes in the society

questions.question: The Congress Ministers resigned from the constituency in the year
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: The Congress formed Ministries in a total of 8 constituencies in the elections held in the year 1937 as per the formula adopted by the Government of India Act 1935. However, after the World War II broke out, Congress Ministers resigned from their posts as British government had announced Indian participation in the war without taking any consensus from Indian leaders

questions.question: Who among the following was the founder of Lodhi Dynasty?
questions.explanation: Bahlol Lodhi was one of the Afghan Sardars. He established himself in Punjab after the invasion of Timur. He founded the Lodhi dynasty in India

questions.question: Who was known as the last ruler of Sayyid Dynasty that ruled Delhi till 1451?
questions.explanation: Alam Shah was the last ruler of Sayyid Dynasty and he descended in favor of Bahlol Lodhi after retirement. Thus began the Lodhi Dynasty in India in the year 1451 but was it was confined to Delhi and a few surrounding areas

questions.question: Which Delhi Sultan transferred his capital from Delhi to Devagiri?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Bin Tughlaq was a ruler of the Tughlaq dynasty and he transferred his capital from Delhi to Devagiri in order to rule the Deccan part of the country without being away from it. It was also named Daulatabad but later the capital was transferred back to Delhi

questions.question: Senabhakta was a tax that was introduced during the rule of -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: During the rule of the Gupta kings, the army was to be fed by population of the area through which it used to pass that area. This tax was called Senabhakta

questions.question: Junagarh Rock inscription is related to whom among the following?
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: Junagarh Rock is related to the Gupta King Skandagupta and it is mainly the description of the works and life of the ruler. It is in the form of a prasasti

questions.question: During Gupta Rule, cities were also known as -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: During the Gupta rule, there were various units for administrative purpose and they were divided into Cities, villages, districts and provinces. Bhogapati was the head of the Province whereas the head of district was also known as Vishayapati, the head of vithika was known as vithikapati and the head of gram was known as Gramika

questions.question: Who was the regent during the initial days of ruling of Emperor Akbar?
questions.explanation: Akbar was the eldest son of Humayun and he ascended the throne under the title of Jalaluddin Muhammad Akbar Badshah Gazi at a young age of 14 years at Kalanaur, Punjab under the regency of Bairam Khan

questions.question: Din-I-Ilahi was introduced in the year -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: As a revolt against the orthodoxy and bigotry of religious priests, Akbar proclaimed a new religion named as Din-I-Ilahi in 1581. It was a synthesis of values taken from various religions such as Hinduism, Sikhism etc. Birbal was the only Hindu who embraced this religion

questions.question: Emperor Akbar was buried in which among the following places?
questions.explanation: Emperor Akbar was buried in Sikandara near Agra. He was the real founder of Mughal Kingdom in India. He was the first Mughal emperor who divorced religion from politics.

questions.question: The most important God during the Rig Vedic period was -
questions.explanation: Lord Indra was the main God during the age of the Rig Vedic Age. He is also known as the destroyer of forts. He was known as a rain god and also a warlord. A number of 250 hymns are dedicated to him in the Vedas

questions.question: How many rivers have been mentioned in the Rig Veda?
questions.explanation: Rig Veda mentions 40 rivers out of which the Nadisukta hymn is Rig Veda mentiones about 21 rivers. This includes Ganges in the east and the Kuba or Kabul in the West

questions.question: The Atharva Veda is associated with which among the following Upavedas?
questions.explanation: Every Veda is associated with a upaveda or the auxiliary veda. Shilpaveda or Arthaveda is associated with the Atharvaveda. It is mainly related to the science of craft or wealth i.e. Vishwakarma

questions.question: Which among the following Harappan city is known as a city without a citadel?
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: Chhanhudro is a Harappan city and it is known as a city without a citadel. From this city, inkpot, lipstick, shell ornament maker's shops and bead makers' shops, imprint of dog's paw on a brick etc have been found

questions.question: Every Veda has a text that explains the meaning of the hymns in it. It is known as -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: The Bramhans explain the hymns in the Vedas. They are written in prose and is ritualistic in nature. It means sacrifice. The various sacrifices and rituals have been elaborately discussed in these texts

questions.question: The concluding portion of the Bramhans is called which among the following?
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: Aranyaks are mainly written for the hermits and their students living in the forest areas. Aranya means forest and these texts are the concluding portions of the Bramnhans

questions.question: Vindhyashakti is associated with which among the following dynasties in India?
questions.explanation: The Vakatakas are the most important power that hold sway in most of the parts of the Deccan and the central India after the fall of the Satvahanas and the rise of the Chalukyas. The founder of this dynasty was Vindhyashakti and the rule of this dynasty ended in the 5th Century AD

questions.question: Who among the following built the great Vrihadeshwar Temple at Tanjore?
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: The Chola dysnasty was founded by Vijayalaya and he captured the capital Tanjore in 850 AD. The greatest ruler of this dynasty was Rajaraja and his son Rajendra I. Rajaraja built the famous Vrihadeshwar temple at Tanjore and it is mainly attrinuted to Lord Shiva

questions.question: The Slave Dynasty was founded in the year -
questions.explanation: Qutubiddin Aibak was a slave under Mohammad Ghori and after the death of Ghori, Aibak founded the Slave Dynasty in Delhi in 1206. Because of his benevolent nature, he was given the title of Lakh Baksh or the Giver of Lakhs

questions.question: Which among the following was the total number of members in the Constituent Assembly initially?
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: The members of the constituent assembly were chosen from the electoral representatives from various provinces as was envisaged in the draft of the constituent assembly. The number of members in the assembly was 389 whereas after independence, the number of members was 299 because of the partition of the country

questions.question: During Quit India Movement, parallel government was not found in which among the following places?
questions.explanation: Indian National Congress decided to go for Quit India Movement in 1942 in which the British people were to quit the country. 'Do or die' became the most popular slogan during this movement. Parallel governments were formed in places such as Balia (UP), Satara (Maharashtra), Tamluk (West Bengal), Talcher (Odisha) etc. The government, however, crushed the entire movement

questions.question: Who called the Cripps Mission 'A Post Dated cheque on a crumbling bank'?
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: Cripps Mission was sent to India as a part of the effort of the British government to gain the support of Indian people during the World War II. The mission tried to devise a formula to make a truce between Indian leaders and the British people. However, it failed because there was no mention of full freedom to India in its recommendations and that is why Gandhi called it 'A Post Dated Cheque on a Crumbling bank'

questions.question: First Muslim invasion in India took place in the year of -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: The first Muslim invasion in India took place during the time of Mohammad Bin Qasin who was the chief architect of the invasion. He invaded India in the year 712 AD and conquered Sindh province which became the province of Omayyad Khilafat

questions.question: Who among the following is NOT a part of the 'Three Gems of Tamil poetry'?
questions.explanation: In Tamil poetry, Kambana was very famous and wrote Ramavtaram and his Ramayana is also known as the Kamba Ramayana. Kambana, Kultana and Pugalendi are known as the three gems of Tamil Poetry

questions.question: Who among the following kings adopted the title of 'Adivaraha'?
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: The Pratiharas were a force to reckon with in the South West Rajasthan area of the country during the reign of the Palas. Bhoja or Mihir Bhoja was the most prominent ruler of the dynasty. He was a devotee of Vishnu and was known as Adivaraha

questions.question: During Gupta period, who started the office of Sandhivigrahika?
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: During the reign of Gupta ruler Samudragupta, the office of the Sandhivigrahika first appeared and his amatya Haisena held this title for the first time during his rule

questions.question: Who among the following is the only Gupta ruler to issue Copper coins in the country?
questions.explanation: According to the book Devi Chandragupta written by Visakhadatta, Samudragupta was succeeded by Ramgupta. It is observed that he ruled over a very short period of time and he was only Gupta ruler to have issued Copper coins in the country

questions.question: The tradition of Sangam used to take place in -
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: Sangam was an assembly of Tamil poets held under the royal patronage of the Pandiyan Kings in Madurai. According to tradition, the assembly lasted for 9990 years and was attended by 8598 poets and 197 Pandiyan kings

questions.question: Which among the following was the time period of Milinda who ruled in the 2nd century BC?
options: Other than those given in options
questions.explanation: In the post Maurya period, the Indo-Greeks were the most important and first foreign ruler to have ruled in the North Western India. The most famous Indoa-Greek ruler was Menander or Milinda who ruled during 1656 BC and 145 BC. He was converted to Buddhism by Nagarjuna.
